hero.title: Level up your pickleball game, choose smarter gear, and find people to play with.
hero.lead: Picklary brings together level guides, DUPR self-checks, gear criteria, pro-scene context, and review-first community tools in one pickleball hub.

pathway.label: DUPR pathway 2.0–5.0
pathway.note: Pick a level to see the next skill focus, gear clues, and practice path.

author.title: The editor & columns
author.visitorIntro: Read the columns the editor has put together — shorter, opinionated pieces on where players actually get stuck.

brief.title: The Brief
brief.intro: A periodic, plain-English digest: what's worth knowing in gear, rules, and the competitive scene — in our own words, each item linking to its primary source. We don't republish other people's articles.
brief.disclaimer: Each item is summarised in our own words and links to its primary source. Picklary does not reproduce source text.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: Picklary is email-based. Questions, corrections, and suggestions are all welcome.
contact.note: This form opens a pre-filled message in your own email app. Picklary does not run a server that receives messages, so nothing is stored here.

categories.title: Guides by topic
categories.intro: Every guide lives in one of five topics. Pick where you want to improve.

fallback.notice: This article hasn't been translated yet — showing the original English.

footer.built: An independent, reader-focused pickleball information site.

tool.title: Paddle finder
tool.intro: Answer three quick questions for a starting profile to shop by. This suggests a paddle type, not a specific product — feel always wins, so try before you buy.
tool.q1: What do you want most from a paddle?
tool.q2: At the net, you'd rather have…
tool.profileIntro: Based on your answers, look for:
tool.recControl: a control-oriented paddle — softer feel and a forgiving sweet spot
tool.recSpin: a textured or raw carbon face for extra spin and bite
tool.recPower: an easy-power paddle that pops without a big swing
tool.recHands: a lighter, maneuverable build for quick hands at the net
tool.recStability: a slightly heavier, stable head that holds its line
tool.recGentle: a lower-vibration build (softer core, cushioned grip) to be kinder to your arm
tool.note: A starting point for shopping, not a specific product. Feel is personal — try before you buy.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: That page may have moved or never existed. Try the homepage, or browse the guides.

sitemap.title: Sitemap
sitemap.intro: Every page on Picklary, in one place.

level.indexIntro: Select from 2.0 to 5.0 to get level-matched skills, drills, paddle profile, and recommended guides.

paddles.title: Paddle rankings and finder
paddles.intro: Browse popular paddles by brand, type, shape, price band, level fit, traits, and review signals. Verify current price and approval status before buying.
paddles.approvalNote: Prices, specs, and approved-equipment status can change. Treat this as a research dashboard and verify official retailer and equipment-list pages before purchase.

players.title: World pro player profiles
players.intro: Study major pro players by style, skills, events, paddle line, live DUPR/ranking links, and news-tracking shortcuts.
players.live: Check live DUPR, rankings, results, and news
players.sourceNote: Live DUPR, results, seedings, and news change frequently. The profile is a learning card; use official sources for current numbers.

highlights.title: Highlight Battle
highlights.intro: Organize highlight clips with level and skill tags, then use the sample leaderboard and feedback template to build better clip-review habits.
highlights.uploadTitle: Create a highlight card in your browser
highlights.moderation: This tool does not upload video to a server; it creates a browser-only preview card. Before public sharing, check copyright, likeness, privacy, reporting, and moderation rules.

visual.court: Court zones: serve boxes, net, and kitchen line.
visual.skills: Shot map: dink, drive, third-shot drop, and reset.
visual.paddles: Paddle shapes affect reach, forgiveness, and hand speed.
visual.players: Player cards connect skills, results, DUPR, and news.
visual.highlights: Highlight uploads create feedback and ranking loops.
visual.boards: Pickleball community board for FAQ and Q&A
